name: ING
mission: The entire experience of planning a series of seminars with ING was inspirational. They did an amazing job. The ING team welcomed input along the way which allowed each session to be even better than the one before it. We will continue to work with ING as I am confident that they will continue to provide value in our work.

score_details.score_summary: Islamic Networks Inc shows moderate alignment and limited impact (minor risk deductions applied). Evidence score may reflect limited reporting capacity, not program weakness.

zakat.claim_evidence: The search results for 'The Islamic Networks Group (ING)' describe it as a 'left-progressive advocacy group promoting the interests of Muslims living in the United States'. There is no explicit mention on their website or in the provided search snippets of accepting zakat donations, having dedicated zakat funds or campaigns, or serving the 8 asnaf categories. The information focuses on their advocacy, educational programs, and funding from foundations. (Source: https://www.influencewatch.org/non-profit/the-islamic-networks-group/)

all_citations.claim: Islamic Networks Inc reports a program expense ratio of 39.5% and holds 5.2 months of working capital.
The organization reported total revenue of $688,262.
The mission involves fostering a deeper understanding of Muslims and other faith-based groups through educational outreach.
The organization tracks metrics such as live audience attendees and educator effectiveness ratings.
Programs include interfaith panels and training for Muslim teens to address prejudice.
The organization accepts general charitable donations (Sadaqah) to support its operations.
The organization engages volunteers through its youth program and community testimonials.

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: The organization receives a score of 35/100 due to concerns regarding its program expense ratio and low data confidence, balanced by its long-standing presence in interfaith education.
areas_for_improvement: Low program expense ratio of 39.5% indicates significant non-program spending
Limited data confidence and lack of third-party impact verification
Low directness of impact as a capacity-building organization

dimension_explanations.alignment: As a Sadaqah-eligible organization, it offers a religious and cultural cause for donors interested in interfaith harmony [7].
dimension_explanations.impact: The organization's impact is categorized as capacity-building, focusing on educational reach and educator satisfaction rather than direct service delivery [3][4]. While it reports reaching thousands through live presentations and digital content, the program expense ratio is 39.5%, and there is a lack of independent verification for its long-term outcomes [1][2].

baseline_narrative.summary: Islamic Networks Inc, also known as Islamic Networks Group (ING), is a non-profit organization dedicated to promoting a nuanced understanding of Muslims and diverse faith-based communities [3]. The organization operates specialized programs including the Islamic Speakers Bureau and Inclusion and Belonging Training to address religious and racial prejudice [1][5].

case_against.summary: The primary concern is the <cite id="7">low program expense ratio</cite>, which indicates that less than 40 cents of every dollar directly funds programs.

confidence.notes: Low confidence due to poor program expense ratio and lack of external verification.

alignment.explanation: As a <cite id="25">SADAQAH-ELIGIBLE</cite> organization, it serves a niche in the <cite id="26">religious and cultural cause area</cite> focused on Muslim identity and interfaith peace.
alignment.improvement: To better align with donor needs, the charity could <cite id="27">clearly state its sadaqah-only status</cite> on a dedicated giving page.

credibility.explanation: The organization has a <cite id="1">30-year history</cite> and utilizes an <cite id="20">independent board</cite> for oversight. However, credibility is tempered by <cite id="21">low data confidence</cite> and the lack of a fully public Charity Navigator rating.
credibility.improvement: Credibility would improve by publishing <cite id="22">consistent beneficiary counts</cite> and seeking external impact evaluations.

impact.explanation: Impact is driven through capacity building, reaching <cite id="23">400,000 live audience attendees</cite>. However, the <cite id="7">39.5% program ratio</cite> is well below the <cite id="8">85.3% peer median</cite>.
impact.improvement: The charity should <cite id="24">document a clear theory of change</cite> with explicit causal pathways to better demonstrate its effectiveness.

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who value long-term interfaith bridge-building and educational advocacy within the American Muslim context.
donor_motivations: Want to support educational efforts to counter Islamophobia
Value organizations with a deep 30-year history in the US
Prefer funding interfaith dialogue and youth leadership training
Look for charities with zero fundraising costs
giving_considerations: A significant portion of the budget covers administrative costs
Impact is measured through reach and satisfaction rather than direct poverty relief
ideal_donor_profile.not_ideal_for: Donors seeking high program-to-overhead ratios or those looking to fulfill zakat obligations.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: Grade C reflects strong internal tracking of reach and satisfaction but a lack of independent, third-party impact evaluations.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: ING uses educational presentations and interfaith panels to shift perceptions of Muslims, aiming to reduce prejudice through direct engagement and curriculum resources.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Evidence ensures that educational outreach actually changes long-term attitudes rather than just providing temporary information.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: With a small revenue base of $622,869 and a large national mission, the organization has significant capacity to scale its educational programs.

strengths.detail: The organization spends <cite id="9">$0.00 per $1 raised</cite>, ensuring that donor acquisition costs do not deplete incoming contributions.
operating since 1993, the group has conducted <cite id="13">780 interfaith panels</cite> and trained <cite id="14">200 Muslim teens</cite> in a single year.
The charity maintains <cite id="12">5.2 months of working capital</cite> in working capital, providing a significant buffer against funding fluctuations.

strengths_deep_dive: Islamic Networks Group excels in content delivery and audience engagement. Their educational impact is evidenced by an <cite id="15">educator satisfaction rating of 96%</cite> and an <cite id="16">effectiveness rating of 89%</cite> for their speakers. Beyond live events, their digital footprint is substantial, with <cite id="17">50,000 monthly website visitors</cite> and over <cite id="18">870,000 views of recorded talks</cite>.
rich_narrative.summary: Islamic Networks Inc, commonly known as Islamic Networks Group (ING), is a long-standing educational organization <cite id="1">founded in 1993</cite> to counter prejudice through interfaith dialogue. The charity focuses on <cite id="2">fostering a deeper understanding of Muslims</cite> and other faith-based groups by providing educational resources and training. Their work is primarily centered on capacity building, utilizing an <cite id="3">Islamic Speakers Bureau</cite> and <cite id="4">Inclusion and Belonging Training</cite> to reach diverse audiences. In 2023, the organization reported <cite id="5">total revenue of $622,869</cite>, reflecting a <cite id="6">3-year revenue CAGR of 6.0%</cite>. While the charity has a significant history, its financial structure shows a <cite id="7">program expense ratio of 39.5%</cite>, which is notably lower than the <cite id="8">peer program ratio median of 85.3%</cite>. Despite this, the organization maintains high efficiency in its development efforts, reporting <cite id="9">$0.00 spent per $1 raised</cite>. Their outreach is extensive, with <cite id="10">projected annual reach of 2,000,000</cite> individuals through various digital and in-person platforms. The charity also engages in grantmaking, distributing <cite id="11">$72,117 in domestic grants</cite> to partner organizations in 2023. With <cite id="12">5.2 months of working capital</cite>, the organization demonstrates strong short-term operational stability.
